video_title: Holcim CEO: Concrete, AI and Europe's Housing Crisis | Podcast | In Good Company
gist: Holcim CEO Miljan Gutovic (NBIM owns ~2%) lays out a post-North-America-spin pure-play: green cement/concrete is now >1/3 of sales and margin-accretive, Europe is inflecting as permits turn up against a ~10M-home shortage, Latin America (Peru, Mexico) is the highest-margin growth engine reached via the ~2,500-store Disensa retail network, and AI predictive maintenance targets CHF200M net benefit by 2028 — all on a decentralized 'local for local' model that insulates from tariffs.

insights.claim: Holcim spun off its North American business to let Europe/North America pursue diverging strategies, and the remaining Holcim finished 2025 as the best-performing stock on the Swiss Market Index.
insights.speaker: Miljan Gutovic (Holcim CEO)
insights.so_what: Post-spin Holcim is a focused European/EM/LatAm building-materials pure-play — re-rating already reflected in 2025 outperformance.
insights.quote: We finished 2025 as the best performing stock on Swiss market index

insights.claim: Eco-friendly cement and concrete launched ~5 years ago now represent more than one-third of Holcim's sales, replacing CO2-intensive and expensive raw materials with cheaper, lower-carbon versions.
insights.speaker: Miljan Gutovic
insights.so_what: Green product mix is both a margin lever and a decarbonization lever — supports the 'sustainability drives profitable growth' thesis with a real revenue share.
insights.quote: after 5 plus years, these products represents more than 1/3 of our sales

insights.claim: Europe (Holcim's biggest market) faces a shortage of close to 10 million homes, and building-permit approvals have turned 'very healthy' across key markets in the last two months — the CEO expects a near-term construction recovery.
insights.speaker: Miljan Gutovic
insights.so_what: Permit approvals are a leading indicator for European cement volumes — a watch signal for a demand turn after several soft years.
insights.quote: in the last 2 months, we have seen a very healthy uh permitting approvals across all our key markets

insights.claim: Permitting is the single biggest constraint on European building; the EU's affordable-housing plan plus moves to simplify and speed permitting are the key catalysts to unlock supply.
insights.speaker: Miljan Gutovic
insights.so_what: Regulatory/permitting reform — not rates alone — is the swing factor for European construction-materials demand.

insights.claim: Rising European defense spending will pull through infrastructure (rail and road) spending, and an eventual end to the Ukraine war opens reconstruction demand Holcim can serve from Poland, Romania, Moldova and Hungary.
insights.speaker: Miljan Gutovic
insights.so_what: Defense and reconstruction are second-order cement/aggregates demand drivers — optionality on top of the housing cycle.

insights.claim: Latin America carries Holcim's highest EBIT margins; the CEO points to a booming Peru (mining/agriculture investment, just-closed acquisition) and Mexico as the biggest country, where President Claudia Sheinbaum targets 1.8 million homes in five years and a top-10 economy by 2030.
insights.speaker: Miljan Gutovic
insights.so_what: LatAm is both the margin leader and a politically tailwinded growth market — a key driver of group profitability.
insights.quote: She wants to build 1.8 million homes in the next 5 years

insights.claim: About half of Holcim's Latin American customers are self-builders buying bagged cement; Holcim reaches them through ~2,500 mostly-franchised Disensa retail shops that supply up to 85% of those customers' needs.
insights.speaker: Miljan Gutovic
insights.so_what: The Disensa retail network is a distribution moat in fragmented self-build markets that pure B2B competitors can't easily replicate.
insights.quote: We operate close to 2,500 retail construction shops called Disensa

insights.claim: Holcim targets 200 million Swiss francs in net AI benefits by 2028, primarily via custom predictive-maintenance algorithms that flag impending plant failures so shutdowns and maintenance can be planned — framed as efficiency/upskilling, not layoffs.
insights.speaker: Miljan Gutovic
insights.so_what: A quantified, near-dated cost/uptime lever for a 24/7 heavy-industrial operator — supports margin durability.
insights.quote: this year we came out with the ambitious plan to hit 200 million Swiss francs in net benefits by 2028

insights.claim: Cement is ~8% of global CO2 emissions (more than aviation); Holcim was first in the industry to commit to net-zero by 2050.
insights.speaker: Miljan Gutovic
insights.so_what: Positions Holcim ahead on the regulatory/carbon-cost curve in a sector facing rising CO2 liabilities.
insights.quote: we were the first company now in the industry that committed to net zero by 2050

insights.claim: Holcim gets PAID to take waste materials, processes them into an energy source, and burns them instead of fossil fuels — cutting both cost and CO2, so decarbonization functions as a profit driver rather than a cost.
insights.speaker: Miljan Gutovic
insights.so_what: Reframes ESG capex as margin-accretive — the alternative-fuels economics are a structural cost advantage.

insights.claim: Holcim increased its Europe profit by 430 basis points over five years, with sustainable offering, decarbonization and circular construction cited as key drivers, and says the US political shift on ESG has not changed its strategy.
insights.speaker: Miljan Gutovic
insights.so_what: Hard margin evidence that the sustainability strategy is paying off — and management conviction it persists through the political cycle.
insights.quote: in the last 5 years we have managed to increase our profit in Europe by 430 basis points

insights.claim: Holcim runs a fully decentralized 'local for local' model — sourcing, producing and selling within each market through a slim corporate structure — which the CEO credits for industry-leading EBIT margins.
insights.speaker: Miljan Gutovic
insights.so_what: Local-for-local sourcing structurally insulates Holcim from tariffs and cross-border trade disruption — a resilience feature in a fragmenting trade environment.
insights.quote: We are fully decentralized business. And the reason why our business is local for local. We source everything locally, we produce everything locally and we sell to local customers

insights.claim: The CEO names complacency as the company's biggest enemy and runs management on three rules — control the controllable, eliminate complacency, and relentless internal/external benchmarking.
insights.speaker: Miljan Gutovic
insights.so_what: Signals a cost-discipline, performance-benchmarking culture — relevant to whether margin leadership is sustained through an upcycle.
